pages.text: One sunny morning, <name> was playing in the backyard when a tiny robot rolled out from behind the rose bushes. “Beep-boop!” it said, blinking its little lights.
“Are you here to water the plants?” asked <name> with a grin.

The robot nodded eagerly and pressed a button. Squirt! Juice sprayed everywhere — mango, guava, and orange — splashing all over the flowers and <name>’s shoes! The robot slipped on a banana peel, spun in a funny circle, and chased a bouncing ball, making <name> giggle until his cheeks hurt.
“Oh no! We need to fix you,” said <name>, picking up a small wrench and some colorful tape. He asked the robot, “Do you want water or juice?” The robot beeped happily and jiggled its little arms.
Carefully, <name> tightened a bolt, pressed a switch, and checked all the buttons. Soon, water gushed gently from the robot’s can — just like it was supposed to.
Together, <name> and the robot watered the flowers, tomatoes, and little herb garden. The robot danced in the puddles, and <name> laughed at every silly spin.
By the end of the day, the backyard sparkled, and <name> learned that teamwork and curiosity could solve any problem — even a robot that liked to spray juice!
From that day on, <name> and his new robot friend explored, played, and watered the garden — sometimes adding a little extra juice just for fun.
